Dear Sir,

I'm writing to you because I'm having some trouble with my email account.  I've been trying to organize my inbox and make some changes to my settings, but I've run into a few snags.

When I try to save my new configurations, I get an error message that says "ERROR IN SAVING CONFIGURATON...."  Also, whenever I send a message, I get a message saying "write to sent mail failed," so my sent messages aren't showing up in my sent mail folder.

Rukn Luthra

body:

On 02/10/2007, Robin Barker wrote: > Having implemented this and seen it in action in blead-perl, > I now understand the action-at-a-distance issues. > > I have code that does effectively C: > this doesn't warn because Test::More does C and vars.pm > does C. > > We should aim that C and even > C<{use 5.006;} use 5.10.0;> DO warn. Yes. That was my concern. I reverted your patch in bleadperl. > This could be implemented as bit 0x01000000 in $^H, or as $^H{v_string_ok}. > > A clearer solution (but more obtrusive) would be to introduce a separate warning category (portable_vstring) and C (and above) would do > an implicit C, just as C does > an implicit C. Well, technically those solutions are equivalent, but the %^H one avoids using a further bit in the warning

subject: paper 19  - OVERCAST
body:
Subject: paper 19  - OVERCAST

OVERCAST

This document outlines a novel approach to improving network performance and reliability through the innovative use of "overlay networks." Imagine a network of interconnected nodes strategically placed within existing internet infrastructure. These nodes act as intelligent intermediaries, optimizing data flow and enhancing overall efficiency.

**Benefits of this Approach:**

* **Seamless Integration:**  The system can be gradually implemented without disrupting existing internet infrastructure.  
* **Adaptive Routing:** Data packets can be intelligently routed along the most efficient paths, tailored to the specific needs of each application.
* **Enhanced Resilience:** The network can automatically reroute around any potential failures, ensuring continuous operation.
* **Customization:** The system can be configured to meet the unique requirements of different applications.

**Challenges and Considerations:**

* **Management Complexity:**  While the system offers significant advantages, managing this network of interconnected nodes requires careful planning and expertise.
* **Performance Optimization:**  While the system strives for efficiency, it may not always achieve the same level of performance as solutions implemented directly within routers.
* **Firewall and NAT Compatibility:**  Careful consideration must be given to ensure compatibility with existing firewalls and network address translation (NAT) systems to prevent data loss.

> section would put a large and unnecessary burden on me. In general  
> every
> change of the 2.6 source tree makes it into 3.0. Exceptions to the  
> rule
> is stuff that makes no sense like 3.0 compatibility and warnings.
>
> Thumb rule: Changes, bug fixes and new features in 2.6 are also in 3.0
> except they are outruled by a Python 3.0 feature.
>

Hi John,

I wanted to share some exciting news about a potential partnership opportunity with a promising Australian company, Australian Power and Energy Limited (APEL). They're developing a cutting-edge project in the Latrobe Valley, focusing on sustainable energy solutions. 

Their 1,000 MW power plant will utilize brown coal, a readily available resource in the region, and will also produce a significant amount of clean-burning diesel fuel. This innovative approach to energy production could be a real game-changer for both Enron and APEL.

Don Reid

subject: Re: Purpose for SpamAssassin using MySQL
body:
Subject: Re: Purpose for SpamAssassin using MySQL

Email body:

Rob Mangiafico wrote:
> Picking up on the point of one Bayes DB in MySQL vs. individual ones for 
> each user, is it more effective in an ISP/host environment where you have 
> diverse users to have them all share one Bayes DB with autolearn, or is it 
> better if they each have their own Bayes data in MySQL (per user)?

I've found that for large-scale deployments, a centralized approach to Bayes data management is generally more efficient.  Sharing a single Bayes database across multiple users within a domain allows for better cross-user learning and spam detection accuracy.  

For smaller domains with fewer users, individual databases might be more manageable, but for larger setups, the benefits of a shared database outweigh the potential drawbacks.  This approach promotes a more robust and collaborative spam filtering system.

Daryl

subject: Dear,
body:
Dear,

I'm writing to you because I'm deeply inspired by your country's commitment to education and opportunity.  My name is David Djedjess, and I'm a 23-year-old aspiring medical student from the Congo Republic.  

Unfortunately, due to unforeseen circumstances, I've had to relocate to an asylum camp in Abidjan, Cote d'Ivoire.  The political climate in my home country has made it difficult to continue my studies, and I'm eager to find a stable environment where I can pursue my dream of becoming a doctor.

I've been fortunate enough to inherit a substantial sum of money from my late parents.  This inheritance allows me to cover my educational expenses, but I need assistance with the logistical challenges of relocating to your country.  

I believe your country offers a welcoming and supportive environment for international students, and I'm hoping you might be willing to act as a guardian and help me navigate the process of obtaining a visa and settling in.  

I'd love to learn more about your life and experiences in your country.  Perhaps we could even connect over a video call sometime.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,
David

subject: [SOC100-L:509] (no subject)
body:
Lehua Patacchia Sociology 100 December 11, 2002   The Downfall of Hawaii�s Economy

I remember that homecoming day when the Waialua sugar mill was being closed down. It was a somber occasion, with teachers discussing the impact on our community and asking us how we felt. The closure of the mill was a stark reminder of the challenges facing Hawaii's economy. Many families in Waialua, including my own, had generations of workers at the mill.  As a young student, I didn't fully grasp the gravity of the situation, but I did notice the sadness and uncertainty that hung over the town.

Over the past few decades, Hawaii has been grappling with economic difficulties due to increased global competition. Our reliance on tourism has grown, while traditional industries like agriculture have struggled.  The closing of sugar mills, a result of free trade agreements, has left a void in the local economy.

International markets have become incredibly competitive, with countries like Korea and Indonesia producing goods at lower costs. This makes it difficult for Hawaii to compete in manufacturing. However, free trade also presents opportunities. We can now access goods from around the world, including products traditionally associated with Hawaii, like ukuleles and

subject: Re: [ILUG] iSCSI & Shared Storage Question
body:
LOL, I knew that one was going to pop up :)

Sorry I presumed you'd be aware I was aware of NFS and also Novell's
Storage Services (very cool stuff there) seeing as I am using OES. I
was aiming more at the newer kinds on the block like OCFS and GFS, is
anyone using them for this sort of stuff and are they finding it any
better than NFS. I prob will end up with an NSS or NFS server.

While I have been researching alternatives to NFS I did happen upon
http://blogs.netapp.com/dave/2007/10/is-nfs-a-form-o.html which is
really a interesting post in favour of NFS.  It seems like NFS is a
very robust and well-established solution, and I'm leaning towards it
myself.

Anyhow, I hope I've cleared that up a bit more (Sometimes my hands
don't work the way my brain tells them to and I write something
different)

AJ

Body:

Hi team,

I'm excited to share the latest draft of our online trading GTCS for physical power!  

As you know, we've been working hard to make these terms as flexible as possible to accommodate the diverse needs of our partners.  We've decided to use the EEEI form by incorporation, which we believe will be the most adaptable solution. I've recently come across a unique situation involving a dormant account at my bank.  A client, Mr. Khalil Al Nasser, made a significant deposit several years ago. Unfortunately, due to unforeseen circumstances, he passed away before the funds matured.

subject: UVic alt.* newsgroup information
body:
Subject: UVic alt.* newsgroup information

Jeffrey,

Great news! The University of Victoria has finally come around and lifted the ban on all alt.* groups within Usenet News.  This was a long-fought battle for free speech on campus, and it seems Dr. Sam Scully, Vice-President Academic, has recognized the importance of open discussion.  

I'm reaching out to Dr. Scully to learn more about his decision and the factors that led to this positive change. I'll be sure to share his insights with you as soon as I have them.  

Please feel free to spread the word about this victory for free expression!

Best,

Gianpaolo (John) Panusa 

================= end of repost ==================

I do not represent EFF; this is just me.
